subject: Chemistry
title: Gunpowder Changes the World
description: A 60-second lesson on Gunpowder Changes the World.

hook.text: Chinese alchemists searching for immortality accidentally invented the most destructive mixture in history.

buildup.text: Around 850 AD, they mixed saltpeter, sulfur, and charcoal. It exploded. They called it 'fire medicine.'

discovery.text: The reaction is rapid oxidation: saltpeter provides oxygen, charcoal is fuel, sulfur lowers ignition temperature.

twist.text: China used it for fireworks first. Europeans turned it into cannons and muskets that toppled castles.

climax.text: Gunpowder ended the age of knights and castles. Chemistry reshaped warfare and world power.

punchline.text: Three powders mixed by accident. Empires rose and fell.

quiz.question: What are the three ingredients of traditional gunpowder?
